label: Thumb sprain, x-ray negative, without tendon or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit uncomplicated thumb sprain.
found: Your clinician diagnosed thumb sprain. We did not find red flags requiring a separate emergency pathway today.
home: Rest the sore area as instructed.
Use ice, compression, elevation, gentle motion, or activity limits only as your clinician recommended.
Avoid activity that causes sharp or worsening pain until you are improving.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not combine medicines unless your clinician said it is safe.
return: Worsening severe pain, new swelling, numbness, weakness, color change, or trouble using the limb.
New deformity, inability to use the area, or concern for fracture, dislocation, or tendon injury.
Fever, spreading redness, warmth, drainage, or symptoms not improving as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated thumb sprain.
No emergency red flags, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Red flags requiring a separate ED pathway, unstable vital signs, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
source_audit_notes: medlineplus.sprains_strains supports patient-education framing for thumb sprain and boundary design. Autonomous expansion approved this narrow v1 only when clinician diagnosis is documented and red flags requiring a separate pathway are absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated thumb sprain
red flags requiring a separate pathway absent and documented

label: Biceps tendinitis without rupture or infection red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit uncomplicated biceps tendinitis.
found: Your clinician diagnosed biceps tendinitis. We did not find tendon rupture, infection, neurovascular compromise, or another emergency pathway today.
home: Rest the sore area as instructed.
Use ice, gentle motion, activity limits, or gradual return to activity only as your clinician recommended.
Avoid activity that causes sharp or worsening pain until you are improving.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not combine medicines unless your clinician said it is safe.
return: Worsening severe pain, new swelling, numbness, weakness, color change, or trouble using the arm.
A pop with loss of strength, new deformity, or concern for tendon rupture.
Fever, spreading redness, warmth, drainage, or symptoms not improving as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated biceps tendinitis.
No tendon rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, infection, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Tendon rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, severe pain, neurovascular compromise, infection concern, inability to use the limb, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports tendinitis framing, activity modification, symptom care, and warning signs. Autonomous expansion approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed biceps tendinitis without rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, infection, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated biceps tendinitis
